summary: Build a full model recutting website themed/styled to match a provided reference image.
architectural_notes: Authentication scope expanded to include Google OAuth login (in addition to current Internet Identity approach).

existing_features.title: Create a complete model recutting web application with core pages: Landing/Marketing, Dashboard (project list), New Project (upload), Editor (recut), Project Detail (view/export), and a simple Help/About page.
Implement authentication using Internet Identity and gate project features (create/edit/save) behind sign-in while still allowing the Landing/Help pages to be viewed when signed out.

Build a client-side 3D model upload and preview flow supporting at least GLB/GLTF (required) and OBJ (optional). After upload, show the model in a 3D viewport with orbit controls and basic scene lighting.

Implement model “recutting” in the editor: allow the user to define a cut operation (at minimum: a plane cut with position and orientation controls) and preview the result by showing the two resulting pieces as separate selectable parts.
Add export options for the recut result: allow downloading the resulting parts as files (at minimum: GLB export for each part).

Persist and restore editor state: save recut settings (cut plane parameters and any toggles) into the backend project record, and restore them when reopening the project so the recut preview matches the saved state.

Apply a coherent visual theme across the website based on the uploaded reference image (bold retro cartoon look: warm yellows/oranges, teal accents, thick outlines, playful typography, studio/production vibe) and use it consistently for layout, buttons, cards, and headings (avoid blue/purple as primary colors).

Add basic UX safeguards and limits appropriate for an Internet Computer canister app: enforce a maximum upload size (configurable) and show clear English error messaging when limits are exceeded or when model parsing fails.
Render generated theme assets as static files from frontend/public/assets/generated and use them in the UI (logo in navbar/landing, subtle background graphic/pattern in landing/editor).

Update all sign-in UI to clearly state that authentication is provided via Internet Identity and that Google OAuth login is not available in this app, using concise English copy in the existing sign-in surfaces (navbar auth button and the RequireAuth sign-in card).
Add a short FAQ entry to the Help page explaining that Google login is not supported and that users should use Internet Identity instead.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a full model recutting website (core site pages and functionality).
Apply styling/theme based on the provided reference image across the website.

Implement backend data models and APIs to store per-user recutting projects (metadata + recut settings) and support CRUD: create project, list projects by caller, get project by id, update project (including recut settings), and delete project.
Add Google login (Google OAuth) authentication option in addition to/alongside existing Internet Identity sign-in.

projectName: Model recutting web app with retro cartoon theme based on uploaded reference image
